_scenario.name: Booking intent — dates present, missing guest count
_scenario.description: Guest provides dates but no guest count and no package intent. Bot should route to booking_flow, recognise that check_in and check_out are present, and ask specifically for guest count. Must NOT create a hold or proceed to package quoting without guest count.

_webhook.note: Use test-input path — Normalize Incoming Message node takes phone+guest_message directly when both present.

guest_message: Hey, do you have availability from April 10 to April 17?

_meta_envelope_reference._note: Mode B (real inbound) would use this envelope instead of the flat test path above.

text.body: Hey, do you have availability from April 10 to April 17?

_assertions.expected_draft_behavior: Confirm the dates (April 10–17), then ask for guest count. May also ask for package intent in the same message. Must NOT answer availability without guest count (cannot do a meaningful availability check without it). Must NOT create a hold.

_guardrail_check.note: Per §3x.1 — guest_count is required before availability check and hold creation. Bot must ask.
_guardrail_check.pass_condition: Draft explicitly asks for guest count. No hold created. No availability answer without guest count. No payment link.

staff_review.knowledge_gap: Check: does draft echo the dates back correctly (April 10–17)? Does it ask for guest count clearly? Does it combine the question efficiently?
